zotero.abstractNote: Hierarchical clustering is a fundamental task in data analysis. While most existing algorithms require numerical data (e.g., Euclidean distances), in many applications only comparison queries of the form "is object x closer to y than to z?" are available. In this paper, we provide the first theoretical foundations for comparison-based hierarchical clustering. We propose a general framework that allows us to analyze the sample complexity and recovery guarantees of such algorithms. We show that under a standard model of comparison queries, it is possible to recover the true hierarchical structure of the data with high probability. We also provide a concrete algorithm, prove its consistency, and demonstrate its performance on synthetic and real-world datasets.
